item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This felt like a joke I wasn't getting when I read it, and it turns out it was. I'm fine with amusing "serious" SCPs, but when it feels like a joke I feel it should be a -J. That's why I'm downvoting. If it ever gets redesignated a -J, I'll give it an enthusiastic upvote.

One point. You indicated in comments that objects seen through the windows seem larger, but I don't recall seeing a perspective shift noted in the article. Unless I just overlooked it (likely enough), you might want to add that intended detail.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Should an instance of SCP-1906-1 already be a domestic pet, or in some other respect be unavailable for acquisition by the Foundation via licit means, personnel are authorized to falsify the instance's death.

The Foundation killed my dog! Those bastards.

Also, am I understanding correctly that everything visible through the windows is enlarged by the factor of [window's height]/[dog's height]? It isn't explicitly stated in the article, and some comments above imply that their authors understand differently.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Divorcing the windows from the eyes was a pleasant derailing of expectations. It fit well with and enhanced the general "huh? whaaa?" effect.

+1 because it feels like there is a coherent fridge-logic that defies my/The Foundation's attempts to understand it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's only a matter of time before 1906-1 manifests as one of those trendy "designer" breeds, then it'd be a libra-doodle.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Imagine walking in on that at your local library.

Oh, new sitting area? When'd they put this in? Ugh, nothing but flourescent lights though. I'll just open these curtains and get some natural light………AAAAAAAAAAAAAAAA!!!!!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it. Tone has issues, but still it manages a +1 from me.

item.title: Re: Explanation
item.content:encoded: One of my favorite quotes. I now wish I could upvote this again.

item.title: Re: Explanation
item.content:encoded: It's not pun. It's the opposite of the pun. And you don't think it's weird?

item.title: Re: Explanation
item.content:encoded: I was going to upvote based on sheer weirdness and then I read this. It's not weird for the sake of weird, it's just a bad pun, unexplained.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
An inventive application of L-space physics. I'm a sucker for doggies and libraries, so I give +0.5 to each, for a total of +1.

long-term interdiction or institutional decommission of the library hosting SCP-1906 has been found to result in the death of SCP-1906-1

That makes me wonder whether constant reading in the room would help the dog live longer?

You never know what you can find in a dog…
Bullet Tooth Tony: What do you mean "look in the dog?"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I feel like the tone needs a bit of fixing up, especially in the early description, but it's a solidly neat idea and the explanation makes me like it even more.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'll have you know that after reading this I've been trying to come up with a suitably punny name for each instance of SCP-1906-1.

And I have nothing.

Anyway, +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Library inside a dog?

I can dig it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hell of a strange concept.
This kind of strange, to me, feels right.
You have my vote. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Weird as hell, but very intriguing and unique. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Heh, I remember this from ages ago in chat, and I still like the idda. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The vet cannot see any small windows in the dog. The Foundation personnel who are clearly visible through the windows are the veterinarians.

You can sit in front of the window, talking to the vet on speakerphone, as he probes and pokes the dog's face and ribs. You'll see super-mega-closeup views of his fingers. He won't see anything.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I thought it would be awfurry cool, except there are some niggles that bother the crap out of me and obscured the actual idea.

For example, "dog's-eye view" makes me stop and pawse, but I can't sniff out another way of making it work.

Veterinary examination of instances of SCP-1906-1 reveals no anomalies, even when Foundation personnel are clearly visible through the windows.

Does this mean the vet cannot see any windows in the dog? Or that the vet can see the foundation personnel through small windows in the dog but doesn't detect anything particularly weird in the dog's anatomy to compensate? This sentence is a little ruff and could use some context.

I also don't understand why the thing has to disappear whenever it dies. Why can't it just immediately switch to another dog? Why does the room have to switch locations? Throw me a bone, here. It seems more like covering one's bases for the sake of covering one's bases than providing something that'll add to the overall concept. But I may be barking up the wrong tree.

Other than that, please make the concept clearer and less hung-up on details. I think it'd be pawsome then.

+ show block
– hide block

Every single one was puntentional.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is really cool. As a suggestion, this could use a picture of the effect-all it would need is photoshopping together a picture of a college library with a low-level view of an ordinary room. (I'm afraid I don't have photoshop skillz, sorry, and I no longer have any good reason to be in a college library.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is full of wtfux. That said, happily +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I actually liked the explanation, but I don't like the article. It doesn't read engagingly. Could use more work.

Downvoted for now, but I'll reread later.

EDIT: Three years later, I like it well enough now. That fucking joke, though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Made sense to me even before reading the explanation :P I like it.

item.title: Re: Explanation
item.content:encoded: Still doesn't do anything for me.

item.title: Re: Explanation
item.content:encoded: Makes perfect sense.

item.title: Explanation
item.content:encoded:
There's a joke, often attributed to Groucho Marx, but apparently it's not actually his.

Outside of a dog, a book can be your best friend.

Inside of a dog, it's too dark to read.

So I started thinking… what if that were not the case? What if, inside of a dog, there was a tastefully-furnished, well-lit reading room?

And once I had that utterly ludicrous idea, I decided to see where it could go if I played it completely seriously.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I

I don't know.

What?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is…. certainly a thing. A very bizarre, though clearly thought out, thing.

I don't even know how I feel like voting on this. I'm gonna stick with no vote.

Man, this… this thing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't know what the fuck I just read in a bad way.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That, um, you just, um, yeah. Huh. You wrote…huh. Yeah.

"Dog's-eye view" isn't clinical; just estimate the height a dog would see the world from or estimate a range for that same height. Upvoted because good lord I wouldn't have thought of this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Uh… what the fuck?

E: I really don't get it, and the part about how it's connected to the dog is not entirely clear.
